short_text: Stokes to Joshua Little, Caught by Buttler!! Ireland have been bowled out for 157. A duck for Little and Stokes has a wi
full_text: Stokes to Joshua Little, Caught by Buttler!! Ireland have been bowled out for 157. A duck for Little and Stokes has a wicket as asll - back of a length and just outside off, Little was looking to run it down towards third man, gets a thin edge and it's a straightforward catch to Buttler.

short_text: Stokes to Delany, 1 run, fuller length delivery outside off, squeezed square on the off-side for a single
full_text: Stokes to Delany, 1 run, fuller length delivery outside off, squeezed square on the off-side for a single

short_text: Sam Curran to Fionn Hand, Bowled!! Two wickets in the over and Curran continues to have fun in the death overs. Came fro
full_text: Sam Curran to Fionn Hand, Bowled!! Two wickets in the over and Curran continues to have fun in the death overs. Came from round the wicket, angled one back into the right-hander and then got it to straighten - Hand had backed away on the drive and missed it completely, the ball rattles into the top of middle and the zing bails light up, so does England's mood at the 'G'.

short_text: Sam Curran to Delany, 3 runs, nicely played, a bad fumble from Wood and Delany is back for the third - back of a length
full_text: Sam Curran to Delany, 3 runs, nicely played, a bad fumble from Wood and Delany is back for the third - back of a length and at the stumps, he backs away and runs it to short third man, where Wood lets the ball go through and then has to chase it all the way to the ropes

short_text: Sam Curran to Delany, no run, Curran comes from round the wicket - bowls one into the pitch and beats Delany's swing, th
full_text: Sam Curran to Delany, no run, Curran comes from round the wicket - bowls one into the pitch and beats Delany's swing, through to the keeper

short_text: Sam Curran to Fionn Hand, 1 run, slower ball following Hand as he came down the pitch, pushed in front of cover and Hand
full_text: Sam Curran to Fionn Hand, 1 run, slower ball following Hand as he came down the pitch, pushed in front of cover and Hand is off for a quick single

short_text: Sam Curran to McCarthy, Bowled!! Doesn't matter now though, Curran has his man - kept it full, kept it straight and hits
full_text: Sam Curran to McCarthy, Bowled!! Doesn't matter now though, Curran has his man - kept it full, kept it straight and hits the middle-stump, was a full-toss as McCarthy skipped down the pitch, missed it completely and has the base of his middle-stump pegged back. Ireland continue to lose wickets and lose momentum.

short_text: Sam Curran to McCarthy, 2 runs, on a good length and on the pads, clipped in front of square on the on-side, takes on th
full_text: Sam Curran to McCarthy, 2 runs, on a good length and on the pads, clipped in front of square on the on-side, takes on the fielder and comes back for the second. A better throw from Livingstone and he'd have been well short

short_text: Livingstone to McCarthy, 1 run, flighted delivery on off, pushed into the gap behind square on the off-side, brings up
full_text: Livingstone to McCarthy, 1 run, flighted delivery on off, pushed into the gap behind square on the off-side, brings up

short_text: Livingstone to Mark Adair, Caught by Sam Curran!! Livingstone is having a day to remember, this is a big bonus for Engla
full_text: Livingstone to Mark Adair, Caught by Sam Curran!! Livingstone is having a day to remember, this is a big bonus for England, a 3rd wicket for the part-time leg-break bowler - flighted delivery on the leg-stump line, Adair bends down low, swipes high and straight towards deep mid-wicket, no real power behind the shot and Curran does the rest, running in from the deep.

short_text: Livingstone to Mark Adair, 2 runs, Adair comes down the pitch, plays with soft hands and nudges one in front of mid-wick
full_text: Livingstone to Mark Adair, 2 runs, Adair comes down the pitch, plays with soft hands and nudges one in front of mid-wicket, scampers hard and is back for the second

short_text: Livingstone to Delany, 1 run, flighted on the leg-stump line, pushed down to long-on for a single
full_text: Livingstone to Delany, 1 run, flighted on the leg-stump line, pushed down to long-on for a single

short_text: Livingstone to Mark Adair, 1 run, tossed up on the leg-stump line, punched down to long-on for a single
full_text: Livingstone to Mark Adair, 1 run, tossed up on the leg-stump line, punched down to long-on for a single

short_text: Livingstone to Delany, 1 run, the leg-break around off, pushed into the gap wide of cover for a single
full_text: Livingstone to Delany, 1 run, the leg-break around off, pushed into the gap wide of cover for a single

short_text: Mark Wood to Delany, leg byes, 1 run, , banged in short and Delany took his eyes off the ball as he ducked under - but i
full_text: Mark Wood to Delany, leg byes, 1 run, , banged in short and Delany took his eyes off the ball as he ducked under - but it didn't bounce as much as he expected, hits the back of the helmet and lobs down to long leg for a  leg bye

short_text: Mark Wood to Delany, , , fuller length delivery outside off - Delany leans across, opens the face of the bat and then dr
full_text: Mark Wood to Delany, , , fuller length delivery outside off - Delany leans across, opens the face of the bat and then drives it backward of square on the off-side. Curran got across from deep point, is slightly lazy in bending down and lets the ball hit the ropes

short_text: Mark Wood to Mark Adair, 1 run, just short of third man, Woakes was the fielder - ran across to his right and then slid
full_text: Mark Wood to Mark Adair, 1 run, just short of third man, Woakes was the fielder - ran across to his right and then slid forward, but can't get to the ball. Full and well outside off, Adair backed away and then went hard on the drive, sliced it behind square on the off-side

short_text: Mark Wood to Campher, Caught by Buttler!! A soft dismissal and Ireland are losing their way at the moment - Wood shorten
full_text: Mark Wood to Campher, Caught by Buttler!! A soft dismissal and Ireland are losing their way at the moment - Wood shortened the length and angled one down the leg-side, Campher shuffled across and was looking to help it on its way, gets a thin edge and Buttler does the rest, moves swiftly across to his left and completes a fine two-handed catch.

short_text: Mark Wood to Campher, , handy runs, this is so so brave from Campher - shuffled right across the stujmps, used Wood's pa
full_text: Mark Wood to Campher, , handy runs, this is so so brave from Campher - shuffled right across the stujmps, used Wood's pace and then lapped it very fine, runs away to the long leg boundary, despite the fielder being very fine

short_text: Mark Wood to Delany, 1 run, short of a length and angling into the pads, clipped behind square on the on-side for a sing
full_text: Mark Wood to Delany, 1 run, short of a length and angling into the pads, clipped behind square on the on-side for a single

short_text: Livingstone to Delany, 1 run, very full and at the stumps, eased down towards long-on for a single
full_text: Livingstone to Delany, 1 run, very full and at the stumps, eased down towards long-on for a single

short_text: Livingstone to Delany, no run, the hat-trick ball and it's another very full ball, Delany gets his bat down and jams it
full_text: Livingstone to Delany, no run, the hat-trick ball and it's another very full ball, Delany gets his bat down and jams it back at the bowler

short_text: Livingstone to Dockrell, Bowled!! One brings two and - very full and at the stumps, brings out a lazy drive from Dockrel
full_text: Livingstone to Dockrell, Bowled!! One brings two and - very full and at the stumps, brings out a lazy drive from Dockrell, the feet went nowhere and he ended up yorking himself, crashes into the base of middle and Livingstone finds himself on a hat-trick.

short_text: Livingstone to Balbirnie, Caught by Hales!! Balbirnie has holed out to deep backward square leg - end of a fine innings
full_text: Livingstone to Balbirnie, Caught by Hales!! Balbirnie has holed out to deep backward square leg - end of a fine innings from the Irish skipper, flighted delivery outside off, he shuffled right across and then nailed the sweep, didn't bother to keep it down, and didn't get the placement right either, straight down the throat of Hales, who doesn't drop those. Much needed wicket for England, Balbirnie could have caused some serious damage in these last few overs.

short_text: Livingstone to Campher, 1 run, tossed up slower and just outside off, Campher backs away and cuts it square behind point
full_text: Livingstone to Campher, 1 run, tossed up slower and just outside off, Campher backs away and cuts it square behind point for a single

short_text: Livingstone to Campher, , , pushed through by Livingstone - Campher shuffles across, uses the pace on the ball and laps
full_text: Livingstone to Campher, , , pushed through by Livingstone - Campher shuffles across, uses the pace on the ball and laps it very fine, runs away across the fast outfield

short_text: Sam Curran to Balbirnie, no run, Curran shortens the length, hits the deck hard and gets the ball to bounce over Balbirn
full_text: Sam Curran to Balbirnie, no run, Curran shortens the length, hits the deck hard and gets the ball to bounce over Balbirnie's cross-batted slog,

short_text: Sam Curran to Balbirnie, 2 runs, this is good cricket from Balbirnie - Curran followed him with a low full-toss on the p
full_text: Sam Curran to Balbirnie, 2 runs, this is good cricket from Balbirnie - Curran followed him with a low full-toss on the pads, clipped behind square on the on-side and he's back for the second

short_text: Sam Curran to Balbirnie, no run, good comeback from Curran - short of a good length and angling across the right-hander,
full_text: Sam Curran to Balbirnie, no run, good comeback from Curran - short of a good length and angling across the right-hander, Balbirnie has a stab at that and is beaten by the angle

short_text: Sam Curran to Balbirnie, , , Balbirnie is in some mood today - back of a length and at the stumps, he reads the length e
full_text: Sam Curran to Balbirnie, , , Balbirnie is in some mood today - back of a length and at the stumps, he reads the length early, swivels across and then thumps the pull over square leg, went high and over the ropes, a 87m hit

short_text: Sam Curran to Balbirnie, 2 runs, nicely played, fuller length delivery outside off - Balbirnie backs away and then carve
full_text: Sam Curran to Balbirnie, 2 runs, nicely played, fuller length delivery outside off - Balbirnie backs away and then carves the drive over cover - Hales does well to get across and keeps them down to a couple

short_text: Sam Curran to Campher, 1 run, full and follwing Campher as he came down the pitch, off the pad and rolled to the off-sid
full_text: Sam Curran to Campher, 1 run, full and follwing Campher as he came down the pitch, off the pad and rolled to the off-side for a single

short_text: Adil Rashid to Balbirnie, 2 runs, flighted delivery on off, Balbirnie makes room and drives it to the left of long-off f
full_text: Adil Rashid to Balbirnie, 2 runs, flighted delivery on off, Balbirnie makes room and drives it to the left of long-off for a couple of runs

short_text: Adil Rashid to Campher, 1 run, half-an-appeal for LBW, was it pad first? No, a big inside edge on the cut from Campher -
full_text: Adil Rashid to Campher, 1 run, half-an-appeal for LBW, was it pad first? No, a big inside edge on the cut from Campher - pushed through by Rashid and Campher was making room for the cut

short_text: Adil Rashid to Balbirnie, 1 run, a 40-ball half-century for Balbirnie, , flighted around off and it's worked behind squa
full_text: Adil Rashid to Balbirnie, 1 run, a 40-ball half-century for Balbirnie, , flighted around off and it's worked behind square on the on-side for a single

short_text: Adil Rashid to Campher, 1 run, Campher brings out the sweep, shuffles right across, rolled his wrists over and then swep
full_text: Adil Rashid to Campher, 1 run, Campher brings out the sweep, shuffles right across, rolled his wrists over and then swept it backward of square on the on-side for a single

full_text: Adil Rashid to Balbirnie, 1 run, flighted on off, Balbirnie pushes it in front of cover for a single

short_text: Adil Rashid to Campher, 1 run, tossed up on off, eased down towards long-off for a single
full_text: Adil Rashid to Campher, 1 run, tossed up on off, eased down towards long-off for a single

full_text: Mark Wood to Balbirnie, no run, good length on off, dabbed towards backward point

full_text: Mark Wood to Campher, 1 run, short pitched outside off, cut late towards third-man

short_text: Mark Wood to Campher, no run, back of a length and Campher is a bit late on his pull. The ball also bounces more than ex
full_text: Mark Wood to Campher, no run, back of a length and Campher is a bit late on his pull. The ball also bounces more than expected to strike Campher flush on the grill of his helmet. Looks okay but we'll have a slight delay as the physio comes out for a check on his batter

short_text: Mark Wood to Campher, , full and quick on off, Campher slashes hard over backward point for a boundary
full_text: Mark Wood to Campher, , full and quick on off, Campher slashes hard over backward point for a boundary

full_text: Mark Wood to Campher, , back of a length but ends down the leg side

short_text: Mark Wood to Tector, Caught by Buttler!! Edged and gone! On a length outside off, another tentative poke from Tector and
full_text: Mark Wood to Tector, Caught by Buttler!! Edged and gone! On a length outside off, another tentative poke from Tector and this time it gets his outside edge. His poor run with bat continues.

short_text: Mark Wood to Tector, no run, back of a length outside off, Tector pokes at it but can't connect
full_text: Mark Wood to Tector, no run, back of a length outside off, Tector pokes at it but can't connect

short_text: Adil Rashid to Balbirnie, Tucker Run Out!! Unlucky, unlucky Tucker! Pitched up and a tad quicker, Balbirnie pushes it ba
full_text: Adil Rashid to Balbirnie, Tucker Run Out!! Unlucky, unlucky Tucker! Pitched up and a tad quicker, Balbirnie pushes it back to the bowler. Rashid gets a finger onto it before it crashes into the non-striker's stumps and Tucker is well short. Umpire doesn't even review as England break the partnership!

short_text: Adil Rashid to Tucker, 1 run, Tucker stepped out for a big slog. Lucky that his inside edge goes towards short fine and
full_text: Adil Rashid to Tucker, 1 run, Tucker stepped out for a big slog. Lucky that his inside edge goes towards short fine and not onto the stumps

full_text: Adil Rashid to Balbirnie, 1 run, slider onto the pads, glanced towards deep mid-wicket

full_text: Adil Rashid to Tucker, 1 run, tossed up and straight, paddle swept to deep backward square leg

full_text: Adil Rashid to Balbirnie, 1 run, tossed up on middle, driven down the ground to long-on.

full_text: Adil Rashid to Balbirnie, 2 runs, tossed up on off, Balbirnie steps out to drive to the left of long-off. Quick running there to complete the second

full_text: Woakes to Balbirnie, 1 run, back of a length on the body, tapped towards short mid-wicket for a single to retain strike. Smart cricket from Balbirnie. 18 off that Woakes over!

short_text: Woakes to Balbirnie, , now a maximum! Woakes persists with short pitched bowling and strays too far leg on this one. Bal
full_text: Woakes to Balbirnie, , now a maximum! Woakes persists with short pitched bowling and strays too far leg on this one. Balbirnie stays still and lifts his pull over fine leg for a six

full_text: Woakes to Balbirnie, , short pitched on the body, Balbirnie read it early and slogged his pull superbly over Sam Curran who was a bit off his line at deep mid-wicket

full_text: Woakes to Balbirnie, , back of a length straying down leg, Balbirnie gets some bat this time on his pull that runs away to the fine leg boundary

full_text: Woakes to Balbirnie, , fuller but strays down the leg side as Balbirnie danced down to play at it. Buttler makes a good diving stop to his left

full_text: Adil Rashid to Balbirnie, no run, Balbirnie shuffles away and Rashid pushes one quicker outside off. A swing and a miss

full_text: Adil Rashid to Balbirnie, , poor start from Rashid. Loose, wide full toss that Balbirnie slashes over the backward point fielder for a boundary

full_text: Sam Curran to Tucker, , stunning! Dances down to use the pace of the bowler and lifts his drive effortlessly all the way for a six over long-on. Totally risk free and cashing in on the conditions

full_text: Mark Wood to Balbirnie, 2 runs, full and quick on off, Balbirnie gets a toe-edge on his defensive push that goes towards third-man. A mild fumble from Sam Curran there allows the Irish the second run

full_text: Mark Wood to Tucker, 1 run, back of a length straying down leg. Tucker toe ends his pull towards the fine leg fielder. Would have been wide had he left it, but now he'll have to be happy with that single

full_text: Mark Wood to Balbirnie, leg byes, 1 run, back of a length on the body, strikes Balbirnie on the hips towards the left of the keeper for a leg bye

full_text: Mark Wood to Balbirnie, no run, nice reply! Bouncer banged in short at full pace, Balbirnie ducks down

full_text: Mark Wood to Balbirnie, , superb! Balbirnie steps away to drive this outside off pitched up delivery over the cover point fielder for a boundary. That ball was 151 clicks!!

full_text: Mark Wood to Balbirnie, no run, back of a length and straight, tucking up Andrew Balbirnie who defends back to Wood

full_text: Woakes to Tucker, , pace off back of a length on off. Lorcan Tucker waits to cut it just over the cover point fielder. Hales the man who may have got a hand to it, but can't save four!

full_text: Woakes to Tucker, no run, pace off length ball on off. Tucker advances forward but has to check his shot and makes no connection on his defensive stroke

full_text: Woakes to Balbirnie, 1 run, back of a length on off, dabbed towards short third

full_text: Woakes to Tucker, 1 run, pace off on the pads, glanced towards deep mid-wicket

full_text: Woakes to Tucker, , what a shot! Quick and full at the stumps, Lorcan Tucker makes a late shuffle to play a superb lap shot that goes over the short fine fielder. One bounce and into the fence

full_text: Woakes to Balbirnie, 1 run, back of a length on the thigh pad, tucked towards mid-wicket

full_text: Mark Wood to Tucker, no run, back of a length outside off, glides past Tucker' tentative defence

short_text: Mark Wood to Balbirnie, , Mark Wood errs horribly with his line as he darts one on a length well down the leg side. Butt
full_text: Mark Wood to Balbirnie, , Mark Wood errs horribly with his line as he darts one on a length well down the leg side. Buttler does brilliantly with an almost goalkeeper like dive to his left. Then there are overthrows as the fielder at the non-striker's end as the man backing-up fumbles the throw, Stokes it was and he has to chase it down

short_text: Mark Wood to Balbirnie, no run, good length on off, whizzes past Andrew Balbirnie's outside edge in a flash
full_text: Mark Wood to Balbirnie, no run, good length on off, whizzes past Andrew Balbirnie's outside edge in a flash

short_text: Mark Wood to Tucker, 1 run, 151 clicks! Mark Wood generating some steam here with a back of a length delivery on the bod
full_text: Mark Wood to Tucker, 1 run, 151 clicks! Mark Wood generating some steam here with a back of a length delivery on the body. Lorcan Tucker just about fends it towards short mid-wicket

short_text: Mark Wood to Stirling, Caught by Sam Curran!! 150 clicks, back of a length! Really troubling Stirling who tried to give
full_text: Mark Wood to Stirling, Caught by Sam Curran!! 150 clicks, back of a length! Really troubling Stirling who tried to give himself room for another big slash. This time the edge carries straight into the hands of Sam Curran at third-man. England have got the danger man.

short_text: Mark Wood to Stirling, no run, back of a length on off, a swing and a miss for Stirling
full_text: Mark Wood to Stirling, no run, back of a length on off, a swing and a miss for Stirling

short_text: Mark Wood to Stirling, , extra bounce from a back of a length delivery on off. Stirling looks to slap it over the infiel
full_text: Mark Wood to Stirling, , extra bounce from a back of a length delivery on off. Stirling looks to slap it over the infield but top-edges this one towards third-man. Sam Curran the fielder at the fence who tries to throw it back into the field as he was tumbling over the ropes. However, he made contact with the boundary at the time of release

short_text: Woakes to Balbirnie, , shot! Balbirnie shows some courage as he shuffles away to give himself room. Woakes plays into hi
full_text: Woakes to Balbirnie, , shot! Balbirnie shows some courage as he shuffles away to give himself room. Woakes plays into his hands with a pitched up delivery outside off. Driven over the cover fielder for a boundary after a couple of bounces

short_text: Woakes to Balbirnie, no run, length ball that strikes the pads of Balbirnie who misses his clip. Would have strayed down
full_text: Woakes to Balbirnie, no run, length ball that strikes the pads of Balbirnie who misses his clip. Would have strayed down leg though, no real appeal from the English

short_text: Woakes to Balbirnie, no run, full and quick on off, driven uppishly but just short of the cover point fielder
full_text: Woakes to Balbirnie, no run, full and quick on off, driven uppishly but just short of the cover point fielder

short_text: Woakes to Stirling, 3 runs, fuller and quick on off, Stirling gives himself room and tonks it over the infield. Slices i
full_text: Woakes to Stirling, 3 runs, fuller and quick on off, Stirling gives himself room and tonks it over the infield. Slices it so won't get a boundary but they'll run three.

short_text: Woakes to Stirling, no run, bounced out of nowhere! This is more like the MCG pitch from Sunday, back of a length and wh
full_text: Woakes to Stirling, no run, bounced out of nowhere! This is more like the MCG pitch from Sunday, back of a length and whizzes past Stirling's outside edge as he tried to defend off the backfoot

short_text: Woakes to Stirling, , pitched up but well outside off. Stirling takes a swipe but misses as the umpire wides
full_text: Woakes to Stirling, , pitched up but well outside off. Stirling takes a swipe but misses as the umpire wides

short_text: Woakes to Stirling, , full and quick outside off, Stirling drops his bat down in time on his slice as the ball races awa
full_text: Woakes to Stirling, , full and quick outside off, Stirling drops his bat down in time on his slice as the ball races away to the square third-man boundary

short_text: Stokes to Balbirnie, no run, on a length and inward movment from off. Surprises Balbirnie who wasn't ready for the shape
full_text: Stokes to Balbirnie, no run, on a length and inward movment from off. Surprises Balbirnie who wasn't ready for the shape back in as he gets hit high on the thigh pad after failing to tuck it away

short_text: Stokes to Balbirnie, no run, a tad fuller outside off, shaping away. Balbirnie gives it a good old Test match leave
full_text: Stokes to Balbirnie, no run, a tad fuller outside off, shaping away. Balbirnie gives it a good old Test match leave

short_text: Stokes to Balbirnie, no run, on a length and straight, Balbirnie defends back to the bowler
full_text: Stokes to Balbirnie, no run, on a length and straight, Balbirnie defends back to the bowler

short_text: Stokes to Balbirnie, 2 runs, back of a length and slightly wider, punched off the backfoot to the right of third-man for
full_text: Stokes to Balbirnie, 2 runs, back of a length and slightly wider, punched off the backfoot to the right of third-man for a couple of runs

short_text: Stokes to Stirling, 1 run, short pitched again. Stirling slices his big cut shot that lands safely behind backward point
full_text: Stokes to Stirling, 1 run, short pitched again. Stirling slices his big cut shot that lands safely behind backward point

short_text: Stokes to Stirling, no run, back of a length with a bit of away shape from off. Stirling shuffles closer to defend it in
full_text: Stokes to Stirling, no run, back of a length with a bit of away shape from off. Stirling shuffles closer to defend it into the pitch

short_text: Delany to Moeen Ali, , Moeen has decided this is the over to target. Flighted outside off, Moeen slices it over extra co
full_text: Delany to Moeen Ali, , Moeen has decided this is the over to target. Flighted outside off, Moeen slices it over extra cover and Adair gets there from long-off, but fails to make the stop

short_text: Delany to Moeen Ali, 2 runs, quicker and outside off, Moeen cuts square of the wicket and scampers back for the second
full_text: Delany to Moeen Ali, 2 runs, quicker and outside off, Moeen cuts square of the wicket and scampers back for the second

short_text: Delany to Moeen Ali, , whoa! That's a fantastic shot from Moeen. Fraction short and drifted across, Moeen does not try t
full_text: Delany to Moeen Ali, , whoa! That's a fantastic shot from Moeen. Fraction short and drifted across, Moeen does not try to go across the line, but instead flat-bats it straight down the ground and clears the rope comfortably

short_text: McCarthy to Moeen Ali, leg byes, 1 run, ouch! Moeen is surprised by the extra bounce on the back of a length delivery, h
full_text: McCarthy to Moeen Ali, leg byes, 1 run, ouch! Moeen is surprised by the extra bounce on the back of a length delivery, he drops his hands and the ball hits him on forearm before rolling to point

short_text: McCarthy to Moeen Ali, , off-pace delivery trying to follow Moeen but sprays it down leg, wide called
full_text: McCarthy to Moeen Ali, , off-pace delivery trying to follow Moeen but sprays it down leg, wide called

short_text: McCarthy to Moeen Ali, , shot! Moeen makes room and gets under the back of a length delivery, pumps it over mid-off and
full_text: McCarthy to Moeen Ali, , shot! Moeen makes room and gets under the back of a length delivery, pumps it over mid-off and the ball races away

short_text: McCarthy to Moeen Ali, no run, short ball angled across, Moeen drops his hands and allows it to pass
full_text: McCarthy to Moeen Ali, no run, short ball angled across, Moeen drops his hands and allows it to pass

short_text: McCarthy to Livingstone, 1 run, fullish delivery and Livingstone mistimes the drive to mid-off, off the mark with a quic
full_text: McCarthy to Livingstone, 1 run, fullish delivery and Livingstone mistimes the drive to mid-off, off the mark with a quick single

short_text: McCarthy to Livingstone, no run, good length ball around off, Livingstone goes hard on the drive and ekes out an inside
full_text: McCarthy to Livingstone, no run, good length ball around off, Livingstone goes hard on the drive and ekes out an inside edge onto the pad

short_text: McCarthy to Malan, Caught by Fionn Hand!! This is a huge wicket. Yes, Malan was struggling to time the ball but he had g
full_text: McCarthy to Malan, Caught by Fionn Hand!! This is a huge wicket. Yes, Malan was struggling to time the ball but he had got himself in and had an idea about the surface. Short of length angled across, Malan moves across his stumps to play the pull and is deceived by the extra bounce, the ball hits the higher part of the bat and balloons towards third man, Hand runs to his right and takes a good sliding catch.

short_text: Delany to Moeen Ali, , ruins the over. Flighted delivery but it is overcooked, Moeen clears his front leg and drills it
full_text: Delany to Moeen Ali, , ruins the over. Flighted delivery but it is overcooked, Moeen clears his front leg and drills it straight of long-on

short_text: Delany to Malan, 1 run, flatter, fraction short and outside off, Malan cracks it in front of square and Dockrell tidies
full_text: Delany to Malan, 1 run, flatter, fraction short and outside off, Malan cracks it in front of square and Dockrell tidies up

short_text: Delany to Moeen Ali, 1 run, comes down the track and Delany throws it wider of off-stump, Moeen reaches and toe-ends the
full_text: Delany to Moeen Ali, 1 run, comes down the track and Delany throws it wider of off-stump, Moeen reaches and toe-ends the drive to long-off

full_text: Delany to Malan, 1 run, now Malan sweeps one on the bounce to the fielder, shuffles across and picks out deep square leg

full_text: Delany to Moeen Ali, 1 run, flighted outside off, Moeen fetches the slog sweep and hits on the bounce to deep mid-wicket

full_text: Delany to Malan, 1 run, tossed up and Malan goes for the big slog sweep, ends up dragging it to deep square leg

full_text: Joshua Little to Malan, 1 run, short and bowled into the surface, Malan waits and pulls to deep backward square

short_text: Joshua Little to Moeen Ali, leg byes, 1 run, I0$ short of length delivery around leg, Moeen misses the tuck and the ball
full_text: Joshua Little to Moeen Ali, leg byes, 1 run, I0$ short of length delivery around leg, Moeen misses the tuck and the ball must have brushed something on the way to Tucker, bounces in front of him and he cannot glove it cleanly

short_text: Joshua Little to Moeen Ali, , slower ball sprayed down leg, Moeen attempts to tickle but can't reach it
full_text: Joshua Little to Moeen Ali, , slower ball sprayed down leg, Moeen attempts to tickle but can't reach it

short_text: Joshua Little to Malan, 1 run, short of length angled in, Malan tries to help it along the way but gets a top-edge onto
full_text: Joshua Little to Malan, 1 run, short of length angled in, Malan tries to help it along the way but gets a top-edge onto his chest and the ball rolls backward of square leg

short_text: Joshua Little to Malan, , unlucky! Short of length and not much width for Malan to free his hands, he still goes for the
full_text: Joshua Little to Malan, , unlucky! Short of length and not much width for Malan to free his hands, he still goes for the pull and the under-edge escapes past the stumps, beats Tucker to his right and races away

short_text: Joshua Little to Malan, no run, back of a length around off, Malan is cramped for room and stabs it back down the pitch.
full_text: Joshua Little to Malan, no run, back of a length around off, Malan is cramped for room and stabs it back down the pitch. He gestures to Moeen that it was the cutter but replays show a seam-up delivery

full_text: Joshua Little to Moeen Ali, 1 run, I0$ good length ball on middle, Moeen flicks it to fine leg

full_text: Dockrell to Moeen Ali, 1 run, flatter and leg-lined, Moeen tucks it square on the leg-side and gets off the mark

short_text: Dockrell to Harry Brook, Caught by Delany!! Finally they catch one and Dockrell has a wicket. Adair should be a relieved
full_text: Dockrell to Harry Brook, Caught by Delany!! Finally they catch one and Dockrell has a wicket. Adair should be a relieved man as the drop catch does not cost Ireland much. This is a half tracker and Brook nails the pull to deep mid-wicket, he middled it alright but the long square boundaries at MCG did not take it over the rope, Delany steadies himself and reverse cups it safely.

short_text: Dockrell to Malan, 1 run, worked behind square on the leg-side and Malan has to be content with a single
full_text: Dockrell to Malan, 1 run, worked behind square on the leg-side and Malan has to be content with a single

short_text: Dockrell to Malan, no run, flatter and drifted across, Malan sits down to sweep and is deceived by the extra bounce, the
full_text: Dockrell to Malan, no run, flatter and drifted across, Malan sits down to sweep and is deceived by the extra bounce, the ball bounces off his arm and onto his helmet

short_text: Dockrell to Malan, 2 runs, Dockrell affords a smile but he should have had two in two. Malan makes room and slaps it tow
full_text: Dockrell to Malan, 2 runs, Dockrell affords a smile but he should have had two in two. Malan makes room and slaps it towards sweeper cover, Delany runs in and gets both hands but does not hang on. Not as easy as Adair's but that still should have been taken

short_text: Dockrell to Harry Brook, 1 run, That's a sitter put down from Adair. Fraction short and Brook pulls it to long-on, Adair
full_text: Dockrell to Harry Brook, 1 run, That's a sitter put down from Adair. Fraction short and Brook pulls it to long-on, Adair gets around to his left and overruns the ball, tries to take it over his head and makes a mess

short_text: Fionn Hand to Harry Brook, 1 run, full and outside off, Brook throws his bat on the drive and the outside edge flies to
full_text: Fionn Hand to Harry Brook, 1 run, full and outside off, Brook throws his bat on the drive and the outside edge flies to third man, just a single

short_text: Fionn Hand to Harry Brook, 2 runs, shuffles across and nurdles backward of square leg, Brook wanted two from the offset
full_text: Fionn Hand to Harry Brook, 2 runs, shuffles across and nurdles backward of square leg, Brook wanted two from the offset and he races back to complete it. Better throw might have had him in trouble

short_text: Fionn Hand to Harry Brook, , wayward delivery from Hand, sprays the short ball down leg and Brook has nothing to do with
full_text: Fionn Hand to Harry Brook, , wayward delivery from Hand, sprays the short ball down leg and Brook has nothing to do with it

full_text: Fionn Hand to Harry Brook, no run, on a length and close to off, Brook angles it to backward point

short_text: Fionn Hand to Harry Brook, , much needed boundary for England and Brook. Fullish delivery and this does not swing, Brook
full_text: Fionn Hand to Harry Brook, , much needed boundary for England and Brook. Fullish delivery and this does not swing, Brook lofts through the line and it is a clean connection, one bounce and over the ropes

short_text: Fionn Hand to Harry Brook, no run, the ball is still doing enough to keep the Irish pacers interested. Good length ball
full_text: Fionn Hand to Harry Brook, no run, the ball is still doing enough to keep the Irish pacers interested. Good length ball around off, Brook prods forward and is tentative in his steer and the ball shapes away to beat the outside edge

full_text: Fionn Hand to Malan, 1 run, back of a length and Malan is hurried on the pull, mistimes and the ball lands safely near mid-wicket

full_text: Fionn Hand to Malan, , swinging down leg from yorker length, Malan attempts to flick but can't lay anything, wide called

full_text: Delany to Harry Brook, 2 runs, fraction short and Brook pulls in front of square, Malan pushes for the second and makes it in comfortably

full_text: Delany to Malan, 1 run, keeps it tight on off-stump, Malan nudges it to the vacant mid-wicket pocket

full_text: Delany to Malan, , half tracker but luckily for Delany it is way outside off, no chance for Malan to reach it

full_text: Delany to Harry Brook, 1 run, quicker and around off, Brook works it to long-on

full_text: Delany to Harry Brook, no run, three dots and pressure is building. Drifting in and Brook kneels to play the sweep, the ball hits him on the glove and drops near his feet

full_text: Delany to Harry Brook, no run, flatter delivery on off-stump, Brook hangs back and punches it to Delany

full_text: Delany to Harry Brook, no run, comes down the track and pushes it off the inner half to mid-wicket

full_text: McCarthy to Harry Brook, 1 run, backs away and still does not have the room to free his hands, settles with a knock to sweeper cover

full_text: McCarthy to Harry Brook, no run, makes room and is followed by a well-directed bouncer, Brook takes it on but the ball climbs over the top-edge

full_text: McCarthy to Malan, 1 run, short and cramped for room, Malan is rushed on the pull and mistimes it to mid-wicket

full_text: McCarthy to Malan, no run, missed out there, did Malan. Short and ample width outside off, Malan shuffles and goes hard on the cut, beaten on the top-edge

full_text: McCarthy to Malan, , swinging down leg, Malan does not meet with the glance and wide signalled

full_text: McCarthy to Harry Brook, 1 run, good length ball and Brook works it to mid-wicket, runs the first one hard and has to settle with that

full_text: Delany to Harry Brook, 1 run, quicker and at the stumps, Brook is rooted to the crease and bunts it in front of long-on

full_text: Delany to Malan, , streaky! Very full delivery and Malan tries to squeeze the drive through the off-side, but instead gets an outside edge that runs away fine and beats a chasing McCarthy to the fence

full_text: Delany to Harry Brook, no run, comes down the track and the length is dragged back, defended to cover

full_text: Fionn Hand to Malan, 2 runs, short of a length and just outside off - Malan swivels across and pulls it over square leg, back for the second

full_text: Fionn Hand to Malan, leg byes, 2 runs, too straight from Hand, on the pads and Malan missed the flick, brushed the pads and rolled past the keeper, back for a couple of leg-byes

full_text: Fionn Hand to Malan, no run, on a good length and some more late inward seam movement - Malan has it covered though as he blocks it

full_text: Fionn Hand to Malan, , Hand loses his line and spills one well down leg, called as a wide

short_text: Fionn Hand to Harry Brook, 3 runs, short and wide of off, Brook rides the bounce and clatters it over backward point, th
full_text: Fionn Hand to Harry Brook, 3 runs, short and wide of off, Brook rides the bounce and clatters it over backward point, the ball slows down near the ropes and it's hauled back in, Brook is off and running with three

short_text: Fionn Hand to Harry Brook, no run, short of a good length and seaming away from outside off - drew Brook into a loose cu
full_text: Fionn Hand to Harry Brook, no run, short of a good length and seaming away from outside off - drew Brook into a loose cut and beat him on the outside edge

short_text: Fionn Hand to Stokes, Bowled!! Hand has a wicket with his first legitimate delivery in the WC, ? Nips away with the angl
full_text: Fionn Hand to Stokes, Bowled!! Hand has a wicket with his first legitimate delivery in the WC, ? Nips away with the angle, drags Stokes forward and then seams in late, bursting through the yawning bat-pad gap and then going on to crash into the top of middle-stump. That's stuff made of dreams, Hand is wheeling away in joy, he's got a big wicket, the big scalp of Stokes and England find themselves in some trouble now.

short_text: Fionn Hand to Stokes, , Hand starts his T20WC campaign with a poor wide, spilled it well down leg
full_text: Fionn Hand to Stokes, , Hand starts his T20WC campaign with a poor wide, spilled it well down leg

short_text: Mark Adair to Stokes, 1 run, on a good length and seaming back into the left-hander, Stokes was backing away and gets an
full_text: Mark Adair to Stokes, 1 run, on a good length and seaming back into the left-hander, Stokes was backing away and gets an inside edge, dropped down at his feet and they're off for a quick single

full_text: Mark Adair to Malan, 1 run, back of a length and well wide of off, dabbed down towards third man for a single

full_text: Mark Adair to Malan, no run, another ripper - the angle causing trouble for Malan, shapes back in and then nips away with the seam, dragged him into a poke and beat him on the outside edge

short_text: Mark Adair to Malan, no run, nicely bowled - on a good length and in that channel just outside off, nips away late and b
full_text: Mark Adair to Malan, no run, nicely bowled - on a good length and in that channel just outside off, nips away late and bursts past Malan's outside edge

full_text: McCarthy to Malan, , McCarthy spills one full and down leg-side, called as a wide

full_text: McCarthy to Stokes, 1 run, on a good length and at the stumps, Stokes shuffled across and clipped it past square leg for a single

full_text: McCarthy to Malan, 3 runs, , full and wide from McCarthy - Malan leans across, plays with an open face and finds the gap past backward point with the drive, long square boundaries at the 'G', a long chase and he's back for the third

short_text: McCarthy to Stokes, 1 run, the inswinger from McCarthy - Stokes comes down the pitch and gets a thick inside edge on the
full_text: McCarthy to Stokes, 1 run, the inswinger from McCarthy - Stokes comes down the pitch and gets a thick inside edge on the drive, a single to mid-on

full_text: McCarthy to Malan, 1 run, on a good length and at the pads, flicked past square leg for a single

full_text: Joshua Little to Stokes, no run, on a good length and in that channel just outside off, left alone

full_text: Joshua Little to Stokes, no run, short of a good length and holding its line outside off, left alone

short_text: Joshua Little to Hales, Caught by Mark Adair!! The dropped chance hasn't cost Ireland a lot and England have lost both o
full_text: Joshua Little to Hales, Caught by Mark Adair!! The dropped chance hasn't cost Ireland a lot and England have lost both openers now. Shortened the length and angled it across the right-hander, Hales was looking to mow it over to the on-side, hit high on the bat and lobbed behind square on the on-side - the ball was swirling in the air, but Adair called for it, kept his eyes on it as he moved to his left at short fine and then gobbles it up. Now, now - can Ireland make the most of this good start?

short_text: Joshua Little to Hales, 2 runs, chance and it has been , came quickly at him, Delany didn't pick it up, went one-handed
full_text: Joshua Little to Hales, 2 runs, chance and it has been , came quickly at him, Delany didn't pick it up, went one-handed to his right and palmed it over - short and wide, had Hales slashing on the cut

short_text: Joshua Little to Hales, no run, much better from Little - shortened the length and angles it away from the right-hander,
full_text: Joshua Little to Hales, no run, much better from Little - shortened the length and angles it away from the right-hander, has Hales frailing at that and is unlucky not to he found the edge

short_text: Joshua Little to Hales, , , fraction too short from Little - Hales swivels across, picks the length and then plays the s
full_text: Joshua Little to Hales, , , fraction too short from Little - Hales swivels across, picks the length and then plays the short-arm jab, placed it wide of mid-wicket and raced away to the ropes

short_text: Mark Adair to Malan, no run, this one goes the other way, - Adair dragged the length back and got it to shape away from
full_text: Mark Adair to Malan, no run, this one goes the other way, - Adair dragged the length back and got it to shape away from the left-hander, Malan poked from the crease and is lucky to have not edged it

short_text: Mark Adair to Malan, no run, , full and nipping back in late, Malan ended up playing all around the ball, past the insid
full_text: Mark Adair to Malan, no run, , full and nipping back in late, Malan ended up playing all around the ball, past the inside edge and he's lucky that the bounce has saved him - swung over the top of off

short_text: Mark Adair to Malan, 2 runs, back of a length and angling across the left-hander, Malan swivels across and then pulls it
full_text: Mark Adair to Malan, 2 runs, back of a length and angling across the left-hander, Malan swivels across and then pulls it over mid-on, hasn't timed that all that well and comes back for the second

short_text: Mark Adair to Malan, no run, on a good length and slanting away from the left-hander, Malan has a good look at that and
full_text: Mark Adair to Malan, no run, on a good length and slanting away from the left-hander, Malan has a good look at that and lets it go

short_text: Mark Adair to Hales, 1 run, short of a length and well wide of off, steered square on the off-side for a single
full_text: Mark Adair to Hales, 1 run, short of a length and well wide of off, steered square on the off-side for a single

short_text: Mark Adair to Malan, 3 runs, that should get Malan going, on a good length and angling across the left-hander - his feet
full_text: Mark Adair to Malan, 3 runs, that should get Malan going, on a good length and angling across the left-hander - his feet hardly moved as he clonked it over mid-on, comes back for the third run

short_text: Joshua Little to Malan, 1 run, Little shortens the length around middle and off, Malan prods across and drops it to the
full_text: Joshua Little to Malan, 1 run, Little shortens the length around middle and off, Malan prods across and drops it to the on-side for a single

short_text: Joshua Little to Malan, no run, short of a good length and at the stumps, Malan hops across, plays close to his body and
full_text: Joshua Little to Malan, no run, short of a good length and at the stumps, Malan hops across, plays close to his body and drops it back at the bowler

short_text: Joshua Little to Malan, no run, on a good length and shaping away from wide of off, Malan covers the line, lifts his bat
full_text: Joshua Little to Malan, no run, on a good length and shaping away from wide of off, Malan covers the line, lifts his bat and lets it go

short_text: Joshua Little to Malan, no run, on a good length and just outside off, Malan shuffles across and defends it back at the
full_text: Joshua Little to Malan, no run, on a good length and just outside off, Malan shuffles across and defends it back at the bowler

short_text: Joshua Little to Malan, , a wild delivery from Little, very full and spraying it miles of off - the keeper has to dive a
full_text: Joshua Little to Malan, , a wild delivery from Little, very full and spraying it miles of off - the keeper has to dive across and in front of first slip to collect it

short_text: Joshua Little to Buttler, Caught by Tucker!! Edged and caught! The perfect start for Ireland - this was full and wide of
full_text: Joshua Little to Buttler, Caught by Tucker!! Edged and caught! The perfect start for Ireland - this was full and wide of off, Buttler saw the width and went hard on the drive, gets a thin edge and Tucker does the rest. Ireland needed to arrest the momentum that England had, and they have done it in the perfect manner possible.

short_text: Joshua Little to Buttler, no run, short of a length and well wide of off, Buttler went chasing after that and is undone
full_text: Joshua Little to Buttler, no run, short of a length and well wide of off, Buttler went chasing after that and is undone by the spongy bounce, bounced in front of the keeper
